CONTENT.Honours: Young BoysSwiss Super League: 2017–18, 2018–19Eintracht FrankfurtUEFA Europa League: 2021–22IndividualSwiss Super League Young Footballer of the Year: 2018–19Swiss Super League Team of the Year: 2018–19
CONTENT.Club career: Sow made his professional debut for Borussia Mönchengladbach on 25 October 2016, in the second round of the 2016–17 edition of the DFB-Pokal, against second-division club VfB Stuttgart. Sow was substituted on in the 88th minute for Lars Stindl. The match finished as a 2–0 home win for Gladbach.In June 2017, Sow returned to Switzerland, agreeing to a four-year contract with Young Boys. The transfer fee paid to Borussia Mönchengladbach was reported as €1.7 million.He was part of the Young Boys squad that won the 2017–18 Swiss Super League, their first league title in 32 years.On 27 June 2019, Sow signed to Bundesliga club Eintracht Frankfurt a contract until 2024.On 4 August 2023, Sow moved to La Liga club Sevilla for a fee €10 million and signed a five-year contract.
CONTENT.Personal life: Sow is the cousin of the female footballer Coumba Sow.
CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 30 March 2024As of match played 15 October 2023
CONTENT.International career: Born in Switzerland, his father comes from Senegal and his mother's Swiss.  He earned his first appearance for the Switzerland national team on 8 September 2018, coming on as a substitute for Steven Zuber in a 6–0 win against Iceland in the UEFA Nations League. In May 2019, he played in 2019 UEFA Nations League Finals, where his team finished fourth. In 2021 he was called up to the national team for the 2020 UEFA European Championship, where the team created one of the main sensations of the tournament reaching the quarter-finals.

SUMMARY: Mohameth Djibril Ibrahima Sow (born 6 February 1997) is a Swiss professional footballer who plays as a defensive midfielder for La Liga club Sevilla and the Switzerland national team.
